title: Testing CPU scaling in Crimson Desert X3D wins, but not by much, and Raptor Lake shines
summary: We tested 20 CPUs in Crimson Desert to see gaming performance. The results show positive performance with Pearl Abyss' BlackSpace engine. Raptor Lake CPUs shine in the tests.
insight: Raptor Lake CPUs outperform others in Crimson Desert gaming tests.

title: Nvidia will only produce one 88-core Vera CPU model Jensen says the company will make billions of dollars from a single SKU
summary: Nvidia will focus on a single Vera CPU model, aiming to generate billions of dollars in revenue. This strategic move emphasizes efficiency and scalability. The company plans to build a multi-billion-dollar CPU business with Vera processors.
insight: Nvidia prioritizes efficiency with a single Vera CPU model SKU.

title: With H200s set to flow into China, Groq is reportedly set to follow Nvidia is allegedly preparing a custom version of inferencing chip to penetrate region
summary: Nvidia's Groq inferencing chips are set to export to China, following H200 approval.
insight: Nvidia expands AI market presence in China with custom chip offerings.

title: AMD claims it had no knowledge of fake Ryzen 5 7430U CPUs in Chuwi laptops Chinese vendor announces recall of products and refunds, PCB manufacturer could be culprit
summary: AMD claims no knowledge of mislabeled Ryzen 5 7430U CPUs in Chuwi laptops. The company condemns mislabeling and warns against unauthorized CPU modifications. Chuwi has initiated a recall and refund program for affected products.
insight: Intellectual property theft and counterfeit products compromise consumer trust and brand integrity

title: AMD reportedly strikes back with Zen 5 refresh to counter Intel's latest Arrow Lake Refresh CPUs 65W Ryzen supercharged with 400 MHz base clock uplift and almost double the TDP
summary: AMD reportedly releases a Zen 5 refresh with Ryzen 7 9750X and Ryzen 5 9650X, featuring a 400 MHz base clock uplift and a significant TDP increase.
insight: Increased performance to counter Intel's Arrow Lake Refresh CPUs with higher TDP.

title: Endgame: What s it Like to build the Noctua-everything PC inside the Antec Flux Pro Noctua-Edition
summary: The Antec Flux Pro Noctua-Edition case allows for a 100% Noctua PC build with off-the-shelf components. This collaboration provides a unique opportunity to explore the feasibility of all-Noctua systems. A PC with Noctua components might offer a distinct performance profile.
insight: All-Noctua system's thermal performance could potentially outshine competition.

title: Score a liquid-cooled Asus ROG 4K gaming PC with a 9800X3D and RX 9070 XT for under $1,900 right now powerhouse rig comes equipped with 32GB of DDR5 RAM and a 1TB SSD
summary: Purchase a high-performance Asus ROG GM700 gaming PC with AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3D CPU, AMD Radeon RX 9070 XT GPU, 32GB DDR5 RAM, and 1TB SSD for $1,899.99.
insight: Unlock ultra-high-quality gaming at a compelling price point.

title: Get 32GB of DDR5 RAM for an effective price of $101 thanks to this Newegg AM5 bundle buy a 9850X3D, premium MSI X870E Max motherboard, and 32GB of V-Color RAM for $1,278.99
summary: Get a high-performance AM5 gaming rig with a 3-item Newegg bundle. The bundle includes an AMD 9850X3D processor, MSI X870E Max motherboard, and 32GB of V-Color DDR5 RAM. This combination can be purchased for an effective price of $101.
insight: Significant savings with bundled components for high-end gaming rigs

title: SK Group chairman says memory chip shortage will last until 2030 wafer supply trails demand by 20%
summary: SK Group chairman predicts memory chip shortage to last until 2030. Chey Tae-won stated that the global shortage will persist for another four to five years due to insufficient wafer supply. This trails demand by 20%.
insight: Memory chip shortage may hinder technological advancements and innovation sustainably

title: Tech hobbyist makes shoulder-mounted guided missile prototype with $96 in parts and a 3D printer DIY MANPADS includes assisted targeting, ballistics calculations, optional camera for tracking
summary: A tech hobbyist created a shoulder-mounted guided missile prototype using $96 worth of parts and a 3D printer, featuring assisted targeting and ballistics calculations, with an optional camera for tracking.
insight: Affordable DIY MANPADS technology raises concerns for global security and defense

title: Bitcoin is so resilient it could survive as much as 90% of the world's undersea cables failing simultaneously study reveals Bitcoin could still be very vulnerable to targeted attacks, however
summary: A study by Cambridge scientists found Bitcoin resilient to 90% undersea cable failure, but vulnerable to targeted attacks.
insight: Bitcoin's resilience masks vulnerability to targeted attacks and strategic disruption.

title: Industrial RS-485/Modbus Raspberry Pi HAT works with OpenPLC, supports 7V-32V DC input
summary: EngineElectronicAccessories introduces an Industrial RS485/Modbus HAT for Raspberry Pi, supporting OpenPLC and 7V-32V DC input. The board features an onboard RS-485 transceiver with TVS diode protection and diagnostics LEDs. It accepts 7V to 32V DC input, eliminating the need for a separate power supply.
insight: Simplified industrial automation with a single, versatile Raspberry Pi solution.

title: Grinn GenioSoM-360 MediaTek Genio 360P LGA system-on-module enables Edge AI in space-constrained applications
summary: Grinn GenioSoM-360 is a compact system-on-module that leverages MediaTek's Genio 360P SoC for edge AI in space-constrained applications, featuring up to 8GB RAM, 64GB eMMC flash, and various interfaces.
insight: Compact AI processing enables edge AI in space-constrained industrial settings.

title: Flexible sensors tested in bedsheets to lessen patient ulcers
summary: Georgia Tech researchers have developed flexible sensors embedded in bedsheets to track pressure points and prevent pressure ulcers in patients. These sensors are connected to a single cable, allowing for real-time data analysis. This innovation aims to reduce the risk of pressure ulcers in hospitals.
insight: Early detection and prevention of pressure ulcers can save lives.

title: Popular Chrome extension "Save Image as Type" was hijacked, impacting over 1 million users
summary: A popular Chrome extension 'Save Image as Type' was hijacked, impacting over 1 million users. Google delisted the extension earlier this month. The group behind the compromise has been linked to other hijacked Chrome and Edge extensions.
insight: Malicious extensions can compromise millions of users' browser security

title: Crimson Desert Benchmark: 40 GPUs Tested
summary: We tested Crimson Desert on 40 GPUs to evaluate performance at 1080p, 1440p, and 4K.
insight: Crimson Desert shows impressive optimization for modern gaming hardware setups.

title: Death Stranding 2 leaks early as unencrypted Steam build spreads online
summary: Death Stranding 2 leaked early as unencrypted Steam build spread online. The leak mirrors a similar trend from the late 1990s when games were pirated before retail release. The leaked build showcases the game's system requirements.
insight: Early game leaks compromise developer's control and revenue

title: New Intel Arc drivers promise faster game loads with shader distribution
summary: Intel's new Arc GPU drivers improve game load times by up to 2x, adding day-one support for Death Stranding 2 and Everwind.
insight: Faster game loads enhance user experience and reduce frustration.

title: Qihoo 360 accidentally exposed a private SSL key, putting its platform at risk
summary: Qihoo 360's 360 Security Claw AI assistant installer exposed a private SSL key, putting its platform at risk.
insight: Exposure of private SSL key can compromise infrastructure security

title: A number of US cities are pulling the plug on Flock Safety's AI cameras
summary: Several US cities are halting use of Flock Safety's AI-powered surveillance systems due to privacy concerns.
insight: Concerns about mass surveillance and data misuse are growing in the US.

title: Gamer recycles VR roller coaster into fully motion-enabled Elite Dangerous cockpit
summary: A streamer turned a childhood dream into a reality by creating a custom Elite Dangerous cockpit that responds to movement feedback from simulation games, using repurposed used machinery.
insight: Immersive gaming experiences can be created with DIY, sustainable projects.

title: Amazon's Jassy now sees AWS hitting $600 billion a year on AI tailwinds
summary: Amazon's AWS is projected to reach $600 billion annually, driven by AI growth.
insight: Amazon now sees AI as a key driver of AWS expansion.

title: Sony releases new PlayStation 3 update nearly 20 years after launch
summary: Sony has released a new PlayStation 3 update, 4.93, a year after 4.92. The update claims to improve system performance. Patch notes remain vague.
insight: Sony continues to provide updates for decade-old console, despite its decline.

title: Tim Cook shuts down speculation about his retirement: "That's a rumor"
summary: Tim Cook has shut down speculation about his retirement, denying any plans.
insight: Leadership stability crucial for Apple's future strategic decisions and growth.

title: Meta is quietly backing away from the metaverse as it pivots to AI
summary: Meta is shifting focus from the metaverse to AI by limiting its VR world to mobile-only experience.
insight: Meta's pivot away from the metaverse signals AI-focused future priorities.

title: Todd Howard discusses Starfield's next big update and says Elder Scrolls VI is still years away
summary: Bethesda is releasing a large free update and paid DLC for Starfield on April 7. This update will also bring the game to PS5. The move aims to improve the game's performance.
insight: Bethesda continues to support and improve Starfield after its 2023 launch

title: Man says wife stole $172 million in Bitcoin after hidden CCTV cameras captured wallet password
summary: A man claims his wife stole $172 million in Bitcoin after discovering hidden CCTV cameras at their home recorded his wallet password. The incident allegedly occurred in August 2023. The case is now in High Court.
insight: Trust issues can lead to major financial losses and marital disputes.

title: A 1TB MacBook Neo? A modder just made it happen
summary: A skilled modder successfully upgraded a MacBook to 1TB storage capacity, documenting the process in a detailed video.
insight: Upgrade feasibility expands storage possibilities in Apple's limited configuration laptops.

title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang says gamers calling DLSS 5 AI slop are "completely wrong"
summary: Nvidia's DLSS 5 technology has faced criticism for its AI-generated appearance. Gamers are unhappy with the uncanny valley effect in certain games. Nvidia's CEO disputes the criticism.
insight: Perceived AI quality affects game immersion and player experience greatly
